page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 48.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and text processing rates placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 31.82 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves 148.45 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in text generation for small LLM models, delivering 433.41 tokens/sec. However, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as large-block uncached memory writes at 57.78 GB/sec, align with mid-range averages.

The c4-highcpu-24 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local disk storage. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a clear tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. It is highly optimized for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, single-threaded data compression, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 260 MB of L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded capabilities, with top-tier text generation speeds of 433.41 tokens/sec for small LLM models and high static web serving throughput of 41.73 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. With a low 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, this instance represents a tradeoff favoring raw compute over memory capacity. It is highly suited for compute-bound tasks, software compilation, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-24 is a compute-optimized instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-threaded and web serving performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, tailored for high-throughput web serving and single-threaded compression workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-focused 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong single-threaded performance and high-throughput static web serving
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and small-model LLM inference
